page: Microsoft Azure E2ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Epsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor with 2 physical cores and a single thread per core. The memory configuration consists of 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Performance benchmarks indicate a split profile. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the average tier, and single-threaded gzip compression achieving strong results. However, multi-core and multi-threaded operations, such as stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, are relatively weak. Memory bandwidth is average for large block read operations but weak for small block reads and large block writes. Passmark CPU and encryption tests register in the poor tier, reflecting the limitations of a dual-core ARM configuration under heavy cryptographic or parallel workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E2ps_v6 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio on an ARM architecture, which typically provides a lower-cost resource profile compared to x86 equivalents with similar memory capacities. The server is suited for memory-sensitive but compute-light workloads. Appropriate applications include small-scale databases, caching layers, light web serving, and basic memory-optimized utility tasks that do not require high parallel processing power or local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure E2ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Epsv6 family, configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor, it offers a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance, highlighted by strong single-threaded gzip compression, though multi-core and cryptographic performance remains weak. This resource profile makes the E2ps_v6 a cost-efficient option for memory-optimized, low-compute workloads such as small databases, caching layers, and lightweight web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E2ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Neoverse-N2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. It is designed for memory-optimized, low-compute workloads requiring Premium Storage capability.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E2ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 2 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and average single-core performance.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine offering a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-sensitive, low-compute workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: ARM64 architecture featuring a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor
2 dedicated vCPUs paired with 16.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core)
No local storage included, but supports Premium Storage integration
Average single-core performance with strong single-threaded compression
Best suited for small databases, caching layers, and light web serving
